summary: Iter37 — contact_ai_ready action for one-tap bulk approve. Backend: new action kind surfaces contacts whose AI-categorized-unreviewed rows all fall in the SAME account (≥3, or ≥2 adaptive when queue thin). Deduped against contact_in_uncat/contact_split. 5/5 pytest cases pass. Frontend: KIND_STYLES emerald chip with ✓ dot renders; pitchFor produces the AI-categorized-as message; AiPanel cleanup-inquiry message includes the account code+name and 'approve' prompt. E2E verified on 812 LLC (50 top_actions, top 10 counts 213/73/53/48/42/42/36/34/33/30, all contact_ai_ready) and Bright Beans (Adobe·3 AI-ready chip visible with adaptive threshold).
critical_code_review_comments: transactions.py:87-112 — the txn loop only inserts contact_id into ai_ready_by_contact when a real category is set, but split_by_contact.add(...) is populated for the same row unconditionally. Both buckets are then filtered separately. Not a bug (dedupe happens later via `already` set), but the tight coupling between the two paths could bite a future refactor. Consider a comment or a single shared derivation.

transactions.py:141 — cname lookup for split_ranked scans all 50k txns per contact (`next(t.get('contact_name') for t in txns if t.get('contact_id')==cidk)`). O(N*M). At 2009 txns×3-5 split contacts it's fine, but on larger books this is a latent hotspot. Cache a contact_id→name map once from the initial loop.

transactions.py:124-126 — adaptive threshold only lowers _thresh_uncat, not _thresh_split or _thresh_ai_ready. Comment says 'thin queues still get useful suggestions' but split/ai-ready remain at 3. On 812 LLC this happens to be fine; on truly tiny books a 2-row Adobe would still be missed. Consider lowering all three symmetrically.

transactions.py:124 — the trigger condition for adaptive drop counts contacts before dedupe, so if all 4 borderline items are dedupe-collisions the final surfaced count could still be <5. Edge case, low impact.
seed_data_creation: Seeded 2 synthetic contacts + 12 txns under seed_tag ITER37_* on the first pro-visible company (Skyward Sparks). Cleaned up in fixture teardown. No residual data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter37 tests seed against companies[0] (Skyward Sparks). 812 LLC verification tests skip if 812 LLC not visible to logged-in user. Live 812 LLC response captured: 50 top_actions with top-10 counts exactly matching main agent's report (213/73/53/48/42/42/36/34/33/30, all contact_ai_ready).
rca of the issue: Iter36 fix limited to raising cap 8→50 + sorting + pinning flagged_batch. On 812 LLC (1859 AI-categorized-unreviewed rows spread across many vendors, only 53 truly uncategorized), NO contact met the contact_in_uncat ≥3 threshold and no vendor was split across ≥3 categories — so top_actions collapsed to just [flagged_batch]. Iter37 adds a third bucket: for each contact, count AI-categorized-unreviewed rows and if they all land in ONE account with count ≥ threshold, surface as contact_ai_ready — a one-tap bulk-approve opportunity. Deduped against the other two so vendors don't compete. Result: 812 LLC now surfaces 50 top-priority approve-in-one-tap opportunities. Verified end-to-end via pytest + live UI (chip renders emerald with ✓, Fix now opens AiPanel with correct account-aware inquiry message).
